notes: Chief volatile monoterpene ketone of Schizonepeta tenuifolia spikes; serves as the primary indicator for quality control in the Chinese Pharmacopoeia

notes: The predominant natural trans-stereoisomer; provides a clean, sweet minty aroma.
Represented as a flat, non-stereospecific structure, used to catalog commercial mixtures of isomers
A terpene glycoside unique to Schizonepeta tenuifolia; glycoside linkage is annotated while ring-substituent configurations remain undefined in structural databases.
A non-volatile monoterpenoid lactone of Schizonepeta tenuifolia spikes possessing a chiral (6R,7aR) configuration.
Process-induced citric acid ester of 5-HMF; formed during the traditional processing of Prunus mume fruit juice.
Primary cyanogenic glucoside in Prunus species; yields antitussive hydrocyanic acid (HCN) upon stereospecific enzymatic hydrolysis.

notes: Flavanone glycoside isolated from the methanol extract of Prunus mume fruits; displays high-affinity radical-scavenging properties.

notes: Highly abundant chiral dicarboxylic acid in unripe fruits of the Rosaceae family; acts as a key acidity regulator.
